channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadian man sentenced for role in U.S. grandparent scam targeting elderly victims
item.description: A 44-year-old Canadian, Stefano Zanetti, has been sentenced to over 15 years in a U.S. prison for his involvement in a multi-million-dollar grandparent scam targeting elderly Americans. Zanetti was ar...

item.title: Leviste denies Recto’s claims, calls them unfounded
item.description: Naglabas ng pahayag si Leandro Leviste bilang tugon sa umano’y mga sinabi ni Ralph Recto, kung saan mariin niyang itinanggi ang mga alegasyon laban sa kanya at tinawag ang mga ito na walang basehan. A...

item.title: PSG Secures Spot Against Arsenal In Champions League Final
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain don waka enter Champions League final after dem manage hold Bayern Munich to 1-1 draw, wey make dem win 6-4 on aggregate. The action happen for Bavaria as PSG score early through O...

item.title: Indian Startup Pronto Raises $20 Million from Lachy Groom After Brief Meeting
item.description: In a rapid turn of events, Indian on-demand home services startup Pronto secured a $20 million investment from prominent Silicon Valley solo investor Lachy Groom after only a 20-minute meeting with fo...

item.title: Australian Woman in Bali in Critical Condition After Accidental Fall
item.description: A 29-year-old Australian woman, Luca Loveday, is in a critical condition in a Bali intensive care unit after a severe accident during a holiday with a friend. Luca collapsed suddenly while standing in...

item.title: Debate grows in the UK over restricting glyphosate use before harvest amid health and environmental concerns
item.description: Campaigners in the UK are urging the government to ban the use of glyphosate as a pre-harvest drying agent, citing concerns about potential risks to human health. Glyphosate, a widely used herbicide, ...

item.title: Patient incident in Canberra Hospital's behavioural unit raises safety concerns
item.description: In Canberra, a patient was initially charged with strangling a nurse in the behavioural assessment unit of the local hospital's emergency department. The charges, which included choking and assault ca...

item.title: Dignitaries Celebrate 60th Birthday of Mrs Boma Ogan in Okrika
item.description: The 60th birthday anniversary of Mrs Boma Ogan, eldest daughter of former Rivers State Governor Chief Rufus Ada George, was a grand event that attracted notable figures from across Nigeria. Former Gom...

item.title: Voices of Chernobyl: The Women Who Cooked for Clean-Up Crews
item.description: Forty years after the Chernobyl disaster, a lesser-known story emerges about the women from Ukraine’s Rivne Nuclear Power Plant who were sent to cook for the clean-up workers at the stricken facility....

item.title: British colonial perceptions of Afghans as criminals in India
item.description: During colonial India, Afghans and Pashtuns were seen very differently by Indians and the British. While cultural depictions like Rabindranath Tagore’s Kabuliwala showed affectionate human connections...

item.title: Nutrien Reports Significant First-Quarter Profit Growth Driven by Fertilizer Sales
item.description: Nutrien, the world’s largest potash producer, reported a substantial increase in first-quarter profits, boosted by strong sales in its nitrogen and phosphate segments. Rising fertilizer prices contrib...

item.title: Flu risk remains high despite lower cases; vaccination urged for vulnerable groups
item.description: Australia experienced its deadliest flu season this century in 2025, with over 1,700 deaths and nearly half a million reported cases. While influenza cases in 2026 are currently about half the number ...

item.title: Personal Assistant of Suvendu Adhikari Killed in North 24 Parganas, Investigation Underway
item.description: On Wednesday night, Chandranath Rath, who was the personal assistant of senior BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari, was tragically shot dead by unidentified assailants in Madhyamgram, North 24 Parganas. Polic...

item.title: Mpumalanga mother gives birth to quadruplets after expecting twins
item.description: Nomcebo Likhuleni, a 30-year-old woman from Buyelani in Mpumalanga, experienced an extraordinary pregnancy, initially thinking she was carrying twins due to family history. After a surprising confirma...

item.title: Nova Scotia Independent Investigators Examine Crash Involving Police Vehicle and Cyclist
item.description: Nova Scotia's Serious Incident Response Team (SiRT) has launched an investigation following a collision between a Halifax police car and a bicyclist. The incident, which occurred in Halifax, has promp...

item.title: Daily Feng Shui Insights for May 7, 2026 by Master Hanz Cua
item.description: On May 7, 2026, Master Hanz Cua shares his daily feng shui guidance for Filipinos seeking balance, good fortune, and harmony in their homes and workplaces. While the original article is brief, it emph...

item.title: ACT government rejects call for independent review of police watchhouse treatment of Aboriginal youth
item.description: The ACT government has declined a key recommendation from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander oversight body calling for an independent inquiry into practices and incidents at the ACT Police Watc...

item.title: Bengal Police Register 200 Cases and Arrest 433 Over Post-Election Violence
item.description: Following the recent elections in West Bengal, the state police have registered 200 cases related to post-poll violence. Authorities have arrested a total of 433 individuals in connection with various...

item.title: Political Turmoil in West Bengal After Shooting of BJP Leader’s Personal Assistant
item.description: In a disturbing development in West Bengal, Chandranath Rath, personal assistant to BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari, was shot dead in Madhyamgram near Kolkata, just two days after the state assembly elect...

item.title: Shreeji Chemicals Closed by Government over Environmental Violations
item.description: The Kenyan government has moved decisively against Shreeji Chemicals Limited after it was found violating environmental laws. During a raid led by Cabinet Secretary Deborah Barasa and accompanied by N...
